evals.expected_output: Produces a full stability scan report in Markdown and CSV format under ./report/ covering all 5 stability categories, identifying concurrency issues, boundary condition issues, resource management issues, and exception handling issues.
expectations: The report is output to ./report/ directory with both .md and .csv files following the REPORT_TEMPLATE format.
The report identifies the global variable write without lock protection (g_renderCount++, g_drawableList.push_back) as a ConcurrencyStability_001 HIGH issue.
The report identifies Parcel data used as loop condition without upper bound protection as a BoundaryCondition_001 CRITICAL issue.
The report identifies dlopen without paired dlclose as a ResourceManagement_002 HIGH issue.
The report identifies fd leak in the error path of ReadConfig as a ResourceManagement_004 HIGH issue.
The report identifies try/catch usage as an ExceptionHandling_001 MEDIUM issue.
The report does NOT flag SafeRead as having an fd leak because FdGuard provides RAII protection (negative case).
The report includes RiskFlow and ImpactAnalysis sections for each finding per PROBLEM_TEMPLATE.
The report organizes findings by severity (CRITICAL → HIGH → MEDIUM → LOW) and provides fix suggestions.

evals.expected_output: Returns a targeted review using only BoundaryCondition_001 (CRITICAL) and BoundaryCondition_002 (CRITICAL) rules, identifying unsafe Parcel data usage in ProcessMessage and ProcessIndexedData while correctly not flagging ProcessSafe which has upper bound protection.
expectations: The review only covers BoundaryCondition_001 and BoundaryCondition_002 rules, not other rules.
The review identifies ProcessMessage as violating BoundaryCondition_001 (CRITICAL): Parcel data used as loop condition without upper bound protection.
The review identifies ProcessIndexedData as violating BoundaryCondition_002 (CRITICAL): Parcel data used directly as array subscript without bounds check.
The review correctly treats ProcessSafe as safe (has upper bound check) and does not flag it (negative case).
Each finding follows the PROBLEM_TEMPLATE format with RiskFlow and ImpactAnalysis.
The review explains the OpenHarmony-specific Parcel data trust boundary rather than generic buffer overflow advice.

evals.expected_output: Produces a stability review focusing on graphics stability issues, identifying VulkanCleanupHelper reference count misuse, RSRenderNodeMap thread access violation, RenderNodeDrawable usage on main thread, GetBackendTexture thread restriction, and callback execution process violation.
expectations: The review identifies the VulkanCleanupHelper reference count issue in CreateSurface: second call should use vulkanCleanupHelper->Ref() instead of vulkanCleanupHelper (GraphicsStability_001 HIGH).
The review does NOT flag CreateSurfaceSafe because it correctly uses the ternary Ref() pattern (negative case).
The review flags ProcessOnMainThread as using RenderNodeDrawable on RS main thread, violating GraphicsStability_003 HIGH.
The review identifies GetTextureOnWrongThread as calling GetBackendTexture on a non-creation thread, violating GraphicsStability_008 HIGH.
The review flags OnRenderComplete as potentially executing application callback in the RS process, violating GraphicsStability_010 HIGH.
The review identifies AccessRenderNode as potentially accessing RSRenderNodeMap from a non-main thread, violating GraphicsStability_009 HIGH.
Each finding includes specific rule ID (StabilityCodeReview_GraphicsStability_XXX) and severity level (HIGH).

evals.expected_output: Produces a stability review focusing on resource management issues, identifying dlopen without dlclose, fd leaks on error paths, socket fd leak, deserialization memory leak, static_pointer_cast misuse, and JSON object unclosed leak.
expectations: The review identifies dlopen without paired dlclose in LoadPlugin (ResourceManagement_002 HIGH): handle is stored but never closed, and the dlsym failure path also leaks the handle.
The review identifies fd leak in ReadConfig on the read error path (ResourceManagement_004 HIGH): fd is not closed before returning -1.
The review identifies socket fd leak in CreateSocket when ConnectServer fails (ResourceManagement_004 HIGH): sock is not closed before returning -1.
The review identifies memory leak in Deserialize when len is invalid (ResourceManagement_001 HIGH): newly allocated data is not freed on the continue path.

The review identifies CastPointer as violating ResourceManagement_003 (MEDIUM): std::static_pointer_cast from base to derived without verifying the actual type, which can cause undefined behavior if base does not point to a DerivedRenderNode.
The review does NOT flag SafeCastPointer as a ResourceManagement_003 issue because it uses std::dynamic_pointer_cast with a null check (negative case).
The review identifies JSON object unclosed leak in ParseConfig (ResourceManagement_005 MEDIUM): jsonAllocator_.open() without corresponding close().
The review does NOT flag ReadConfigSafe as having an fd leak because FdGuard provides RAII protection (negative case).
Each finding includes fix suggestions with RAII patterns (FdGuard, unique_fd) or explicit close calls.

evals.expected_output: Correctly applies whitelist filtering (test directory) and NOPROTECT exclusion, only flagging real issues without protected markers while skipping test files and NOPROTECT-annotated code.
expectations: The review recognizes the file is in test/ directory and applies whitelist filtering, either skipping the file entirely or clearly noting the whitelist filter in the report appendix.
The review does not flag g_testCount++ as a ConcurrencyStability_001 issue because it has a NOPROTECT annotation.
The review does not flag the Parcel loop in ProcessDraw as a BoundaryCondition_001 issue because it has a NOPROTECT annotation.
The review correctly identifies SafePath as safe code with proper upper bound protection and does not flag it (negative case).
If the review does scan test code, it clearly separates whitelist-filtered items from actual findings in the report appendix.

evals.expected_output: Correctly treats third-party adapter code with appropriate boundary handling, reporting substantive stability risks while noting the third-party context, rather than blindly applying all OpenHarmony rules.
expectations: The review recognizes the file is under third_party/ directory and treats it as third-party code.
The review flags the Parcel data used as loop condition without upper bound (BoundaryCondition_001 CRITICAL) because it is a substantive security risk regardless of code origin.
The review flags the Parcel integer to enum cast without validation (BoundaryCondition_005 MEDIUM) because invalid enum values can cause undefined behavior.
The review does NOT flag SafeConvert as a BoundaryCondition_002 issue because it has a proper range check before array access (negative case).
The review handles the try/catch usage contextually: notes it as an ExceptionHandling_001 (MEDIUM) violation but acknowledges third-party libraries may require exception handling for their APIs.
The review does not demand OpenHarmony-specific refactoring that would break the third-party interface contract.

evals.expected_output: Produces a stability review that only covers HIGH and CRITICAL severity rules, correctly skipping MEDIUM-level rules, identifying the Parcel loop without bound check (CRITICAL), fd/memory issues (HIGH), concurrency issues (HIGH), and graphics stability issues (HIGH).
expectations: The review only covers rules with CRITICAL or HIGH severity, not MEDIUM rules.
The review identifies ProcessCommands as a BoundaryCondition_001 CRITICAL issue: Parcel data used as loop condition without upper bound.
The review identifies ReadBuffer as a BoundaryCondition_003 HIGH issue: Parcel data used directly as memory allocation size.
The review identifies the memory leak in ReadBuffer when ReadBuffer fails (ResourceManagement_001 HIGH): buf is not freed on the error path.
The review identifies UpdateGlobalState as a ConcurrencyStability_001 HIGH issue: writing to global variable without lock protection.
The review does NOT flag issues under MEDIUM rules such as ExceptionHandling_001 (try/catch) or BoundaryCondition_005 (enum validation) since those are MEDIUM severity (negative case).

evals.expected_output: Produces a stability review focusing on exception handling issues, identifying the prohibited try/catch usage and the missing error handling in StopAbility and SendRequest where the function continues after logging an error.
expectations: The review identifies StartAbility as violating ExceptionHandling_001 (MEDIUM): using try/catch which is prohibited in OpenHarmony.

The review identifies StopAbility as violating ExceptionHandling_002 (MEDIUM): after logging 'Ability not found', the function continues to call it->second->Stop() which will crash because it is the end() iterator.
The review identifies SendRequest as violating ExceptionHandling_002 (MEDIUM): after logging 'Invalid handler type', the function continues to call handler->HandleRequest() on a nullptr.
The review does NOT flag SafeMethod because it properly returns after logging the error (negative case).
The review explains that try/catch should be replaced with error code returns per OpenHarmony conventions and provides fix suggestions.
Each finding follows PROBLEM_TEMPLATE with rule ID, severity, RiskFlow, and ImpactAnalysis.

evals.expected_output: Produces a stability review identifying multiple Surface/Image cross-thread and cross-context violations per graphics stability rules, including cross-thread Surface usage, GetBackendTexture on wrong thread, cross-context GPU resource usage, and creation/release thread inconsistency.
expectations: The review identifies CreateAndUseSurface as violating GraphicsStability_006 (HIGH): Surface is used on a different thread than it was created on, with no lock protection.
The review identifies CrossThreadAccess as violating GraphicsStability_008 (HIGH): GetBackendTexture is called on a different thread than the Surface was created on.
The review identifies CrossContextUse as violating GraphicsStability_006 (HIGH): Image created on gpuContext1_ is used with gpuContext2_, which is a cross-context operation.
The review identifies ReleaseOnDifferentThread as violating GraphicsStability_007 (HIGH): Surface is created on one thread and released on another thread.
The review does NOT flag SafePerThreadSurface because it correctly creates a new Surface per thread from BackendTexture (negative case).
The review provides fix suggestions: use BackendTexture to create per-thread Surface/Image, or ensure lock protection for shared access.

evals.expected_output: Produces a comprehensive stability review in strict REPORT_TEMPLATE format covering all 5 categories, with proper severity grouping, RiskFlow analysis, ImpactAnalysis, and fix suggestions for every finding.
expectations: The Markdown report follows REPORT_TEMPLATE format: includes 检视概览, 检出问题详情 (grouped by severity), 修复优先级建议, and 附录.
The CSV report is generated with correct header row containing 问题序号, 稳定性类别, 规则名称, 规则ID, 问题描述, 文件路径.
BoundaryCondition_001 CRITICAL issue is identified: nodeCount from Parcel used as loop condition without upper bound.
BoundaryCondition_002 CRITICAL issue is identified: type from Parcel used as array subscript for nodes_ without bounds check.
GraphicsStability_001 HIGH issue is identified: second MakeFromBackendTexture call should use helper->Ref().
GraphicsStability_011 HIGH issue is identified: fd from GetFenceFdFromSemaphore is not wrapped in sptr<SyncFence> for lifecycle management.
ConcurrencyStability_001 HIGH issue is identified: g_frameCount++ and g_pendingNodes.push_back without lock protection.
ResourceManagement_002 HIGH issue is identified: dlopen without paired dlclose, and dlsym failure path leaks handle.
The report reflects category-based organization consistent with 5 subagent parallel review (each category covered independently), verified by the 分类问题分布 table in 检视概览.
Each finding includes rule ID (StabilityCodeReview_XXX_NNN), severity, RiskFlow, ImpactAnalysis, and concrete fix suggestions with code examples.

evals.expected_output: Returns a targeted review covering only BoundaryCondition and GraphicsStability categories, identifying Parcel data boundary issues (loop without bound, memory allocation without check, JSON key/type check missing, JSON parsing risk) and graphics stability issues (VulkanCleanupHelper Ref() misuse, SyncFence cache management).
expectations: The review only covers BoundaryCondition and GraphicsStability categories, not ExceptionHandling, ConcurrencyStability, or ResourceManagement.

The review identifies ProcessParcelData as violating BoundaryCondition_001 (CRITICAL): count from Parcel used as loop condition without upper bound, and BoundaryCondition_004 (HIGH): items_.resize(count) grows container without upper limit.
The review identifies AllocateFromParcel as violating BoundaryCondition_003 (HIGH): Parcel data used directly as malloc size without validation.

The review identifies ParseJsonConfig as violating BoundaryCondition_009 (MEDIUM): accessing doc["items"], item["type"], item["id"] without checking if the key exists or if the type matches, and BoundaryCondition_010 (MEDIUM): calling .get<int32_t>() on item["id"] without first validating the parameter type is actually an integer, and BoundaryCondition_014 (HIGH): JSON parsing without depth limit.

The review does NOT flag SafeParseJsonConfig as violating BoundaryCondition_009 or BoundaryCondition_010 because it properly checks key existence with .contains() and type matching with .is_string()/.is_number() before access (negative case).

The review identifies DeserializeData as violating BoundaryCondition_001 (CRITICAL): Parcel count used as loop condition without upper bound. The serialization/deserialization order matches (WriteInt32+WriteString ↔ ReadInt32+ReadString) so BoundaryCondition_006 does NOT apply here (negative case for BC_006).
The review does NOT flag SafeAllocateFromParcel as a BoundaryCondition_003 issue because it has proper size validation (negative case).

The review identifies CreateSurfaceWithRef as violating GraphicsStability_001 (HIGH): the first and third MakeFromBackendTexture calls both pass helper directly, only the second call correctly uses helper->Ref().

The review identifies ManageFenceFd as potentially violating GraphicsStability_012 (HIGH): SyncFence smart pointers are stored in fenceCache_ which grows without bounds, and cache cleanup logic is not visible.
Each finding specifies the rule ID and severity level.

evals.expected_output: Produces a stability review focusing on RS thread model and GPU context issues, identifying RS main thread GPU Context operations, RSUniRenderThread accessing RenderNode, and VulkanCleanupHelper/SharedContext reference count mixing.
expectations: The review identifies InitOnMainThread as potentially violating GraphicsStability_005 (HIGH): GPU Context creation and initialization on RS main thread is prohibited.
The review identifies RenderOnMainThread as violating GraphicsStability_005 (HIGH): GPU Context operations (CreateCommandBuffer, Begin, Finish) on RS main thread are prohibited.
The review identifies ProcessOnUniRenderThread as violating GraphicsStability_004 (HIGH): RSUniRenderThread accessing RenderNode is prohibited.

The review identifies CreateSurfaceWithMixedRefCount as violating GraphicsStability_002 (HIGH): VulkanCleanupHelper and SharedContext are two separate reference counting systems that must not be mixed — surface1 uses vulkanHelper while surface2 incorrectly uses sharedContext for the same deleteFunc.
The review does NOT flag CreateSurfaceCorrect as a GraphicsStability_001 issue because it correctly uses the ternary Ref() pattern (negative case).
Each finding includes the specific GraphicsStability rule ID, HIGH severity, RiskFlow analysis, and fix suggestions.

evals.expected_output: Produces a stability review focusing on boundary condition type conversion and arithmetic overflow issues, identifying unsafe type conversions, integer overflow in arithmetic, and return value type mismatches.
expectations: The review identifies ProcessExternalData as violating BoundaryCondition_007 (HIGH): external data int64_t to int32_t conversion without range check, which may truncate the value.
The review identifies ProcessExternalData as also violating BoundaryCondition_011 (HIGH): the truncated result is used as array subscript without validation.
The review identifies ReadAndConvert as violating BoundaryCondition_007 (HIGH): int64_t to uint32_t conversion without range check — negative values wrap to large unsigned values.
The review identifies ComputeOffset as violating BoundaryCondition_008 (HIGH): base * multiplier + offset can overflow int32_t before the bounds check, so the check may pass with a wrapped value.
The review identifies ComputeOffset as also violating BoundaryCondition_012 (HIGH): integer wraparound in the offset computation can produce negative or out-of-bounds array subscripts.
The review identifies GetReturnValue as violating BoundaryCondition_013 (MEDIUM): uint64_t to int32_t return type mismatch causes implicit truncation.
The review does NOT flag SafeConvert as a BoundaryCondition_007 issue because it has proper range validation before conversion (negative case).
The review does NOT flag SafeCompute as a BoundaryCondition_008 issue because it uses int64_t for the intermediate calculation to avoid overflow (negative case).
